perturbation.original_text: During the period beginning on the Effective Date and ending on December 31, 2018 (the "Development Term"), each Party (the "Offering Party") agrees to provide to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") a right of first offer with respect to any proposed offer or arrangement, which the Offering Party (or its affiliate) desires to accept, under which the Offering Party or one of its affiliates would acquire the ownership of real property in the Market Area (the "Development Property") on which a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort would operate (a "Development Opportunity"). If the Offering Party is required to offer the Receiving Party a Development Opportunity pursuant to this Section 1, the Offering Party must deliver written notice to the Receiving Party, together with reasonable due diligence information in the Offering Party's possession to enable the Receiving Party to evaluate the Development Opportunity (collectively, the "Offer Notice"). The Receiving Party will have ten (10) business days after receiving the Offer Notice to notify the Offering Party whether the Receiving Party exercises its right of first offer for that Development Opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: During the period beginning on the Effective Date and ending on December 31, 2018 (the "Development Term"), each Party (the "Presenting Party") agrees to provide to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") a right of first offer with respect to any proposed offer or arrangement, which the Presenting Party (or its affiliate) desires to accept, under which the Presenting Party or one of its affiliates would acquire the ownership of real property in the Market Area (the "Development Property") on which a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort would operate (a "Business Prospect"). If the Presenting Party is required to offer the Receiving Party a Business Prospect pursuant to this Section 1, the Presenting Party must deliver written notice to the Receiving Party, together with reasonable due diligence information in the Presenting Party's possession to enable the Receiving Party to evaluate the Business Prospect (collectively, the "Offer Notice"). The Receiving Party will have ten (10) business days after receiving the Offer Notice to notify the Presenting Party whether the Receiving Party exercises its right of first offer for that Business Prospect.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Offering Party" to "Presenting Party" and "Development Opportunity" to "Business Prospect" within Section 1, while not updating these terms consistently throughout the document, creates an in-text contradiction. Later mentions of "Offering Party" and "Development Opportunity" in subsequent clauses now create ambiguity. It is unclear whether the original definition still applies, or if a 'Presenting Party' and 'Business prospect' are now distinct entities. This impacts enforceability, as the obligations tied to the "Offering Party" are now unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the Receiving Party declines its right of first offer with respect to any Development Opportunity, or fails to notify the Offering Party of its decision within the ten (10) business-day period described above, or if Hyatt's affiliate or Playa's affiliate (as applicable) fails to acquire the Development Property within the sixty (60)-day period described above, then the right of first offer with respect to that Development Opportunity shall expire, and the Offering Party thereafter may acquire, develop and/or operate (and/or grant any other person or entity the right to acquire, develop and/or operate) an all-inclusive resort or other business on the Development Property without any restriction under this Agreement, subject to any restrictions under any Existing Franchise Agreement or other agreement between Hyatt (or its affiliate) and Playa (or its affiliate).
perturbation.justification: The change from "Offering Party" to "Presenting Party" and "Development Opportunity" to "Business Prospect" in the changed text, while not updating these terms in the contradicted text, creates a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text refers to "Offering Party" and "Development Opportunity". Because the modified text redefines those terms as "Presenting Party" and "Business Prospect" within the same document without a clear indication that they are intended to be the same, it creates ambiguity regarding which terms apply, leading to conflicting obligations and making enforcement difficult. For example, the contradicted text refers to 'If the Receiving Party declines its right of first offer with respect to any Development Opportunity'. It is now unclear if this refers to the 'Business Prospect' mentioned earlier in the document, or some other entity that fits the description of 'Development Opportunity' using the original terminology.

perturbation.original_text: If the Receiving Party declines its right of first offer with respect to any Development Opportunity, or fails to notify the Offering Party of its decision within the ten (10) business-day period described above, or if Hyatt's affiliate or Playa's affiliate (as applicable) fails to acquire the Development Property within the sixty (60)-day period described above, then the right of first offer with respect to that Development Opportunity shall expire, and the Offering Party thereafter may acquire, develop and/or operate (and/or grant any other person or entity the right to acquire, develop and/or operate) an all-inclusive resort or other business on the Development Property without any restriction under this Agreement, subject to any restrictions under any Existing Franchise Agreement or other agreement between Hyatt (or its affiliate) and Playa (or its affiliate).
perturbation.changed_text: If the Receiving Party declines its right of first offer with respect to any Business Prospect, or fails to notify the Presenting Party of its decision within the ten (10) business-day period described above, or if Hyatt's affiliate or Playa's affiliate (as applicable) fails to acquire the Development Property within the sixty (60)-day period described above, then the right of first offer with respect to that Business Prospect shall expire, and the Presenting Party thereafter may acquire, develop and/or operate (and/or grant any other person or entity the right to acquire, develop and/or operate) an all-inclusive resort or other business on the Development Property without any restriction under this Agreement, subject to any restrictions under any Existing Franchise Agreement or other agreement between Hyatt (or its affiliate) and Playa (or its affiliate).
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation builds upon the first one. By making changes to the paragraph after the first, using the same terms but only on this clause, it adds more to the confusion. Now the definition of what should be done is uncertain between 'Business prospect', 'Presenting party', 'Development opportunity' and 'Offering Party'. As, depending on the clause, the parties will have to comply different definitions of them making it hard to enforce. It causes ambiguity about who is bound by what obligations and under what conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Introduction to Other Opportunities. If a third party (who is not an affiliate of Hyatt) approaches Hyatt during the Development Term with a proposed offer or arrangement, which Hyatt desires to accept, under which the third party would operate a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort in the Market Area, and if that third party has not then already designated a management company to operate that Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort, then Hyatt agrees to provide notice to Playa and introduce Playa to that third party for purposes of enabling Playa (at its option) to negotiate for the opportunity to manage that Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort for that third party. Similarly, if a third party (who is not an affiliate of Playa) approaches Playa during the Development Term with a proposed offer or arrangement, which Playa desires to accept, under which Playa or its affiliate would manage an all- inclusive resort in the Market Area for that third party, and if that third party has not then already designated a brand under which that all-inclusive resort would operate, then Playa agrees to provide notice to Hyatt and introduce Hyatt to that third party for purposes of enabling Hyatt (at its option) to negotiate for the opportunity to provide that third party franchise rights to brand that resort as a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Business Prospect' and 'Presenting Party' while the contradicted text refers to a different process of identifying potential Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort management opportunities by introducing Hyatt and Playa to third parties. The use of different terminology and processes creates a contradiction, as it's unclear which procedure applies in which situation. The change makes the obligations of Hyatt and Playa uncertain, creating a direct conflict. The original text and changed text describe similar business arrangements of other opportunities, but by changing the parties involved (Offering vs Presenting) and the opportunity (Development vs Business Prospect) within the text, there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Introduction to Other Opportunities. If a third party (who is not an affiliate of Hyatt) approaches Hyatt during the Development Term with a proposed offer or arrangement, which Hyatt desires to accept, under which the third party would operate a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort in the Market Area, and if that third party has not then already designated a management company to operate that Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort, then Hyatt agrees to provide notice to Playa and introduce Playa to that third party for purposes of enabling Playa (at its option) to negotiate for the opportunity to manage that Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort for that third party. Similarly, if a third party (who is not an affiliate of Playa) approaches Playa during the Development Term with a proposed offer or arrangement, which Playa desires to accept, under which Playa or its affiliate would manage an all- inclusive resort in the Market Area for that third party, and if that third party has not then already designated a brand under which that all-inclusive resort would operate, then Playa agrees to provide notice to Hyatt and introduce Hyatt to that third party for purposes of enabling Hyatt (at its option) to negotiate for the opportunity to provide that third party franchise rights to brand that resort as a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort.
perturbation.changed_text: Introduction to Other Ventures. If a third party (who is not an affiliate of Hyatt) approaches Hyatt during the Business Prospect Term with a proposed offer or arrangement, which Hyatt desires to accept, under which the third party would operate a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort in the Market Area, and if that third party has not then already designated a management company to operate that Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort, then Hyatt agrees to provide notice to Playa and introduce Playa to that third party for purposes of enabling Playa (at its option) to negotiate for the opportunity to manage that Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort for that third party. Similarly, if a third party (who is not an affiliate of Playa) approaches Playa during the Business Prospect Term with a proposed offer or arrangement, which Playa desires to accept, under which Playa or its affiliate would manage an all- inclusive resort in the Market Area for that third party, and if that third party has not then already designated a brand under which that all-inclusive resort would operate, then Playa agrees to provide notice to Hyatt and introduce Hyatt to that third party for purposes of enabling Hyatt (at its option) to negotiate for the opportunity to provide that third party franchise rights to brand that resort as a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Development Term” to “Business Prospect Term” and “Opportunities” to “Ventures” adds to the confusion. The 'Development Term' is defined in Section 1, but “Business Prospect Term” isn't. This creates confusion as to the actual timeframe for the obligations described in Section 2, thus, directly impacting the section 1 where it defined “Development Term”, and the obligations that the parties have under Section 1 are now uncertain and unenforceable, and is affecting the first perturbation, by creating more confusing about defining the terms
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the period beginning on the Effective Date and ending on December 31, 2018 (the "Development Term"), each Party (the "Presenting Party") agrees to provide to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") a right of first offer with respect to any proposed offer or arrangement, which the Presenting Party (or its affiliate) desires to accept, under which the Presenting Party or one of its affiliates would acquire the ownership of real property in the Market Area (the "Development Property") on which a Hyatt All-Inclusive Resort would operate (a "Business Prospect"). If the Presenting Party is required to offer the Receiving Party a Business Prospect pursuant to this Section 1, the Presenting Party must deliver written notice to the Receiving Party, together with reasonable due diligence information in the Presenting Party's possession to enable the Receiving Party to evaluate the Business Prospect (collectively, the "Offer Notice"). The Receiving Party will have ten (10) business days after receiving the Offer Notice to notify the Presenting Party whether the Receiving Party exercises its right of first offer for that Business Prospect.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Development Term' to 'Business Prospect Term', when 'Business Prospect Term' is undefined, directly contradicts Section 1, which defines 'Development Term'. This creates uncertainty as to the actual timeframe for the obligations described in Section 2, thus, directly impacting the section 1 where it defined “Development Term”, and the obligations that the parties have under Section 1 are now uncertain and unenforceable, because the defined term ('Development Term') is replaced with an undefined term ('Business Prospect Term').
